From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **14th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party** was in session from 1992 to 1997. It held seven plenary sessions. It was preceded by the [13th Central Committee](/wiki/13th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "13th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party"). It was elected by the [14th National Congress of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/14th_National_Congress_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "14th National Congress of the Chinese Communist Party") and in turn elected the [14th Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/14th_Politburo_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "14th Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party").

## Brief chronology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=14th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Brief chronology")]

1. *1st Plenary Session*
   * Date: October 19, 1992
   * Location: [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing")
   * Significance: [Jiang Zemin](/wiki/Jiang_Zemin "Jiang Zemin") was elected [General Secretary](/wiki/General_Secretary_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "General Secretary of the Communist Party of China") and [Chairman of the Central Military Commission](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Central_Military_Commission_(China) "Chairman of the Central Military Commission (China)"). A 22-members [Politburo](/wiki/Politburo_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "Politburo of the Communist Party of China"), a 7-members [Politburo Standing Committee](/wiki/Politburo_Standing_Committee_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "Politburo Standing Committee of the Communist Party of China") and a 5-members [Secretariat](/wiki/Secretariat_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "Secretariat of the Communist Party of China") were elected. [Hu Jintao](/wiki/Hu_Jintao "Hu Jintao") entered the Politburo Standing Committee for the first time as its youngest member.
2. *2nd Plenary Session*
   * Date: March 5–7, 1993
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The meeting approved some economic measures, a program for institutional reform, and lists of nominees for top posts of the [8th National People's Congress](/wiki/8th_National_People%27s_Congress "8th National People's Congress") and the [8th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/wiki/Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference "Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference").
3. *3rd Plenary Session*
   * Date: November 11–14, 1993
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: A *Decision of the CCP Central Committee on Certain Issues in Establishing a [Socialist Market Economy](/wiki/Socialist_market_economy "Socialist market economy") System* was adopted,[[1]](#cite_note-:0222-1): 236–237 fostering the establishment of private enterprises. This clearly echoed [Deng Xiaoping's Southern Tour](/wiki/Deng_Xiaoping%27s_southern_tour "Deng Xiaoping's southern tour") in 1992, when the still-powerful [paramount leader](/wiki/Paramount_leader "Paramount leader") had criticized the slowness of economic reform after the [1989 Tian'anmen Square protests](/wiki/Tiananmen_Square_protests_of_1989 "Tiananmen Square protests of 1989"). In the area of [social security](/wiki/Social_security_in_China "Social security in China"), the Decision called for mandatory individual accounts for each employee.[[1]](#cite_note-:0222-1): 237
4. *4th Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 25–28, 1994
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The meeting was focused on improving Party leadership in the period of [socialism with Chinese characteristics](/wiki/Socialism_with_Chinese_characteristics "Socialism with Chinese characteristics"). It was first asserted that collective leadership based on generational succession was to be enhanced: [Deng Xiaoping](/wiki/Deng_Xiaoping "Deng Xiaoping") was proclaimed as the leader of the "second generation", and Jiang Zemin as the leader of the "third generation". This principle was later laid down in the Party Constitution.
5. *5th Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 25–28, 1995
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: Guidelines for the 9th Five-Year Plan and other socio-economic programs were adopted. [Chen Xitong](/wiki/Chen_Xitong "Chen Xitong"), rival to Jiang Zemin as leader of the dubbed "Beijing clique", was accused of corruption, removed from his posts of Politburo member, Central Committee member, [National People's Congress](/wiki/National_People%27s_Congress "National People's Congress") delegate and party secretary of [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), and arrested. [Defense Minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_National_Defense_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China "Ministry of National Defense of the People's Republic of China") [Chi Haotian](/wiki/Chi_Haotian "Chi Haotian") was appointed CMC vice-chairman. [Deng Xiaoping Theory](/wiki/Deng_Xiaoping_Theory "Deng Xiaoping Theory") was first mentioned in the official communique.
6. *6th Plenary Session*
   * Date: October 7–10, 1996
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: Meeting focused on *"building socialist spiritual civilization"*. The concept of Deng Xiaoping Theory was further elaborated as the Party's basic guideline for the *"[primary stage of socialism](/wiki/Primary_stage_of_socialism "Primary stage of socialism")"*, i.e. [socialism with Chinese characteristics](/wiki/Socialism_with_Chinese_characteristics "Socialism with Chinese characteristics"), and was raised to the same level as [Marxism-Leninism](/wiki/Marxism-Leninism "Marxism-Leninism") and [Mao Zedong Thought](/wiki/Mao_Zedong_Thought "Mao Zedong Thought").
7. *7th Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 6–9, 1997
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: Preparations for the Party's 15th National Congress were made. This was also the first meeting held after Deng Xiaoping's death.
